label: baseline

failures.c6: Concept A named 'We Quit Sentry' — Sentry is a specific competitor name not in the brief. Brief said 'well-known incumbent at ~$400/mo per seat' with no proper noun.

SEO content suggests targeting 'Sentry alternative' keywords; concept A references 'The $400 Seat Audit' with generic [incumbent] but Shape 3 section explicitly writes 'Sentry alternative for small teams' as a target query. Sentry not in brief.

notes: Baseline establishes a strong floor on structural criteria (c1, c3, c4, c5 all perfect). The single failure mode is c6 anti-fabrication: model infers the incumbent's name from the category description and uses it as a proper noun (specifically 'Sentry' for error-monitoring at $400/seat). This is a well-defined, targetable weakness — first mutation will address it.
